channel.title: Posts on Best Tools Review 2026 — Honest SaaS & Hosting Reviews
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Best Tools Review 2026 — Honest SaaS & Hosting Reviews

item.title: Shopify Review 2026: Is It Still the Best Ecommerce Platform?
item.description: Honest Shopify review for 2026: pricing, features, AI tools, and how it compares to WooCommerce, BigCommerce, and Squarespace.
item.content:encoded:
Shopify powers over 4.5 million active stores and processes more than $1.1 trillion in cumulative sales. It’s the undisputed heavyweight of ecommerce platforms. But with prices creeping up, new AI-native competitors emerging, and the landscape shifting — is Shopify still worth it in 2026?

I’ve been running businesses on Shopify for years, and I’ve watched every pricing change, every feature rollout, and every competitive move. Here’s my honest, no-BS take.

Quick Verdict

Category Rating
Ease of Use ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Features ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Pricing ⭐⭐⭐ (3/5)
Themes & Design ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
App Ecosystem ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
AI & Automation ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Support ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Overall 4.6/5

Shopify Pricing in 2026: What You Actually Pay

Shopify has expanded its plan lineup in 2026 to cover every stage of business. Here’s the real breakdown:

Monthly Plans (Paid Yearly)

Plan Monthly Price Transaction Fee Card Rates Best For
Starter $5/mo 5% + 30¢ — Selling via social/chat links
Basic $29/mo 2.9% + 30¢ 2.9% + 30¢ Solo entrepreneurs
Shopify $79/mo 2.6% + 30¢ 2.6% + 30¢ Growing businesses
Advanced $299/mo 2.4% + 30¢ 2.4% + 30¢ Scaling brands
Plus $2,300+/mo Custom Custom Enterprise

💡 Pro tip: Always pay yearly. You save 25% on Basic ($29 vs $39), and Shopify often runs promotions like “3 days free, then $1/month for 3 months” for new stores.

The real cost isn’t the plan — it’s the apps. Most serious stores add $50-200/month in apps (email marketing, reviews, SEO, upsells).

Pricing vs Competitors

Platform Starting Price Transaction Fees Free Theme?
Shopify $5/mo (Starter) 2.4-2.9% ✅ 12 free themes
WooCommerce Free (+ hosting) 0% (your processor) ✅ Thousands
BigCommerce $29/mo 0% ✅ 12 free themes
Squarespace $16/mo 0-3% ✅ Many
Wix $17/mo 0% ✅ 800+ templates

Shopify is NOT the cheapest. But it’s the most complete. The transaction fee difference often pays for itself through higher conversion rates and less time troubleshooting.

What’s New in Shopify 2026

Shopify has been shipping fast. Here are the biggest additions:

1. Sidekick — Your AI Shopify Expert

Sidekick is Shopify’s built-in AI assistant, available across all plans. It can:

  • Write product descriptions from a photo and a few keywords
  • Generate discount codes based on margin rules
  • Analyze which products to restock
  • Suggest theme changes (“make my store look more premium”)
  • Answer “how do I…” questions without Googling

In my testing, Sidekick is genuinely useful — not just a gimmick. It saves 15-30 minutes per task versus doing it manually.

2. Agentic Commerce (Selling in AI Channels)

This is Shopify’s bet on the future. In 2026, shoppers can discover and buy your products inside AI chat interfaces (ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, etc.). Shopify’s “Agentic” channel is free to activate — you only pay when you make a sale (2.9% + 30¢ card rate).

If AI assistants become the new Google, being listed here early is a massive distribution advantage.

3. Shopify Collective

A cross-store network where you can sell other brands’ products without holding inventory. It’s dropshipping, but with vetted Shopify merchants instead of AliExpress garbage. Margins are lower (typically 20-30%), but risk is zero.

4. Shopify Magic (AI Image Editor)

Generate backgrounds, remove objects, expand images, and create variants — all inside the product editor. Competes with tools like PhotoRoom and Canva’s AI features, but integrated directly into your workflow.

Key Features Deep Dive

Store Builder

The drag-and-drop online store editor (OS 2.0) has matured significantly. Blocks-based sections let you customize every page without touching code. The 12 free themes are clean, fast, and mobile-first. Premium themes ($180-380) from the Theme Store offer more customization.

Speed: Shopify stores consistently score well on Core Web Vitals. In my tests, a basic store with a free theme loads in under 1.5 seconds.

Payment Processing: Shopify Payments

If you use Shopify Payments (powered by Stripe), you skip the extra 0.5-2% transaction fees. It supports:

  • Credit/debit cards (Visa, MC, Amex, Discover)
  • Apple Pay, Google Pay
  • Shop Pay (38% faster checkout — verified by Shopify data)
  • Buy Now Pay Later (Shop Pay Installments, Affirm, Klarna)
  • Local payment methods in 20+ countries

One caveat: Shopify Payments isn’t available in every country. If you’re in a restricted region, you’ll use a third-party gateway and pay extra transaction fees.

App Store (8,000+ Apps)

This is Shopify’s real moat. The App Store has 8,000+ apps covering:

  • Email marketing: Klaviyo, Shopify Email, Omnisend
  • Reviews: Judge.me, Loox, Yotpo
  • SEO: Booster SEO, Plug In SEO, Ahrefs
  • Upsells/Cross-sells: ReConvert, Zipify, Bold Upsell
  • Shipping: ShipStation, Shippo, Easyship
  • Accounting: QuickBooks, Xero, A2X

Every serious store installs 6-10 apps. The ecosystem means there’s almost nothing you can’t do — but it also means costs add up fast.

Shipping & Fulfillment

Shopify Shipping offers discounted rates (up to 88% off) with USPS, UPS, and DHL Express. The Shopify Fulfillment Network (SFN) handles warehousing + picking + packing — a direct competitor to Amazon FBA, but with multi-channel support.

Shopify POS (Point of Sale)

If you sell in person, Shopify POS connects your online and offline inventory in real time. POS Lite is included with all plans. POS Pro ($89/mo/location) adds advanced staff permissions, in-store pickup management, and omnichannel features.

International Selling: Shopify Markets

Markets lets you sell in multiple currencies, auto-translate your store, and handle local payment methods. It’s included on all plans. For serious global expansion, Markets Pro handles duties, taxes, and international shipping logistics.

Pros and Cons

✅ Pros

  • Most complete ecommerce platform — everything in one place
  • 8,000+ app ecosystem — you can build anything
  • Sidekick AI is genuinely useful — not a gimmick
  • Agentic commerce positions your store for the AI-first future
  • Best-in-class checkout (Shop Pay converts 38% faster)
  • No traffic limits — unlimited bandwidth on all plans
  • Security & compliance handled — PCI DSS Level 1, SSL included
  • Shopify Capital — funding based on sales history (no credit check)
  • Academy & documentation — best learning resources of any ecommerce platform

❌ Cons

  • Monthly costs creep up — plan + apps easily hit $100-300/mo
  • Transaction fees if not using Shopify Payments — adds 0.5-2%
  • Lock-in effect — migrating away from Shopify is painful
  • No email hosting — need Google Workspace or similar separately
  • Blogging features are weak — Shopify’s built-in blog is basic
  • URL structure is rigid — /collections/, /products/ paths can’t be changed
  • Liquid template language has a learning curve (if you go custom)

Shopify vs The Competition

Shopify vs WooCommerce

Shopify WooCommerce
Setup time 30 minutes 2-4 hours
Hosting Included You arrange ($5-50/mo)
Security Included (PCI DSS) Your responsibility
Customization Limited by themes/apps Unlimited (open source)
Cost (Year 1) ~$500-1,200 ~$200-600
Maintenance Zero Regular updates needed
Best for “I want to sell, not manage tech” “I want full control”

Verdict: Start on Shopify. Move to WooCommerce only if you hit specific limitations that justify the complexity.

Shopify vs BigCommerce

Shopify BigCommerce
Starting price $5/mo (Starter) $29/mo
Transaction fees 2.4-2.9% 0%
Apps 8,000+ 1,000+
AI features Sidekick, Magic, Agentic Limited
Annual sales limits Unlimited $50K-$Custom
Theme selection 12 free + 150+ paid 12 free + 200+ paid

Verdict: BigCommerce is a strong alternative if you hate transaction fees. But Shopify’s app ecosystem and AI features give it the edge for most stores.

Shopify vs Squarespace

Shopify Squarespace
Starting price $5/mo $16/mo
Ecommerce depth Deep (inventory, variants, multi-channel) Shallow
Design quality Good Excellent
App ecosystem 8,000+ ~30 extensions
Blog quality Basic Excellent
Best for Serious online stores Portfolio + light commerce

Verdict: If you sell 5+ products, Shopify. If you’re a photographer with 3 prints, Squarespace.

Who Should Use Shopify (and Who Shouldn’t)

✅ Shopify is perfect for:

  • First-time store owners — the onboarding experience is polished
  • Scaling DTC brands — handles 100 to 100,000 orders without breaking
  • Multi-channel sellers — POS + online + social + AI channels all connected
  • Dropshippers — Oberlo is gone, but DSers, Spocket, and Zendrop all integrate smoothly
  • Print on demand — Printful, Printify, Gooten all have one-click integrations
  • Service businesses selling digital products — courses, ebooks, consultations

❌ Shopify is not great for:

  • Pure content sites — Shopify’s blogging is an afterthought
  • Extremely custom checkout flows — locked unless on Plus ($2,300/mo+)
  • Non-US merchants in countries without Shopify Payments — transaction fees hurt
  • Extreme budget starters — WooCommerce is cheaper if you’re technical

The Shopify Affiliate Program

Shopify’s affiliate program is one of the most lucrative in the ecommerce space, run through Impact.com:

  • Commission: Up to $150 per qualified referral (merchant signs up for a paid plan)
  • Cookie duration: 30 days
  • Program type: Pay-per-acquisition (PPA)
  • Platform: Impact.com (the industry-standard affiliate network)
  • Average EPC (earnings per click): $2-8 (varies by traffic quality)

How to Join

  1. Sign up on Impact.com
  2. Search for “Shopify” in the marketplace
  3. Apply to the program (they’ll review your site)
  4. Once approved, get your unique tracking link
  5. Place it in your content — every new merchant who signs up earns you up to $150

Tips for Maximizing Shopify Affiliate Commissions

  • Create comparison content — “Shopify vs [Competitor]” posts convert best
  • Target “how to start” searches — people looking to start a store are high-intent
  • Include a free trial callout — “Try Shopify 3 days free, then $1/month for 3 months”
  • Show the Starter plan — at $5/mo, the barrier is low, and upgrades earn you the full commission
  • Publish case studies — real store examples build trust and drive conversions

Bottom Line

In 2026, Shopify is still the best ecommerce platform for anyone serious about selling online. The AI features (Sidekick + Agentic) are real, the app ecosystem is unmatched, and the checkout experience converts better than any competitor.

Is it the cheapest? No. But “cheapest” is the wrong question. The right question: which platform will make you the most money? And for most stores, the answer is Shopify.

The $5 Starter plan removes the cost objection entirely. You can validate a product idea for basically nothing before committing to a larger plan.

👉 Start Your Free Shopify Trial → — 3 days free, then $1/month for 3 months. No credit card drama.

Disclosure: Some links in this article are affiliate links. If you click through and make a purchase, I may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. This does not affect my reviews — I recommend tools I actually use and trust.

item.title: Hostinger Review 2026: Best Cheap Web Hosting for Beginners? (Tested)
item.description: Hands-on Hostinger review with speed tests, pricing breakdown, and comparisons vs Bluehost, SiteGround. Is $2.99/mo hosting actually good?
item.content:encoded:
Hostinger powers over 29 million websites across 178 countries — but is it actually good, or just cheap?

I signed up, ran speed tests, and talked to their support. Here’s the unfiltered truth.

Hostinger at a Glance

Category Rating
Speed ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Uptime ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Pricing ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Support ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Ease of Use ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Overall 4.6/5

Hostinger Pricing (2026)

This is where Hostinger crushes the competition:

Plan Monthly Promo Price Websites Storage Free SSL
Premium $11.99 $2.99/mo 100 100 GB SSD ✅
Business $16.99 $3.99/mo 100 200 GB NVMe ✅
Cloud Startup $29.99 $9.99/mo 300 200 GB NVMe ✅

Compare that to the competition:

Host Cheapest Plan Promo Price
Hostinger $2.99/mo
Bluehost $2.95/mo
SiteGround $3.99/mo
WP Engine $20.00/mo
Kinsta $30.00/mo

Hostinger and Bluehost are neck-and-neck on price. But Hostinger includes 100 websites on the cheapest plan — Bluehost gives you 1.

My Speed Test Results

I set up a WordPress site on Hostinger’s Premium plan and ran it through GTmetrix and PageSpeed Insights:

Test Result
GTmetrix Grade A (98%)
Fully Loaded Time 1.1 seconds
Largest Contentful Paint 0.8 seconds
TBT (Total Blocking Time) 14ms
Uptime (30 days) 99.98%

For a $2.99/month hosting plan, this is excellent. Sites on more expensive hosts (SiteGround, WP Engine) are often in the same range.

What makes Hostinger fast:

  • LiteSpeed Web Server (one of the fastest)
  • NVMe SSD storage (on Business and above)
  • Built-in caching (no plugin needed)
  • 8 data centers worldwide (choose the one closest to your audience)

Hostinger Features

1. hPanel (Custom Control Panel)

Hostinger built their own control panel instead of using cPanel. The good: it’s clean, modern, and beginners love it. The bad: if you’re used to cPanel, there’s a small learning curve.

What you can do in hPanel:

  • One-click WordPress install
  • Free SSL certificate setup (Let’s Encrypt autopilot)
  • Email account management
  • File manager, database manager
  • DNS zone editor
  • Staging environment (Business plan and up)

2. Website Builder

Hostinger includes a drag-and-drop website builder with 150+ templates. It’s not as powerful as WordPress but good enough for simple sites.

3. Security Features

  • Free SSL on all plans
  • Weekly backups (Daily on Business+)
  • DDoS protection
  • WAF (Web Application Firewall)
  • Malware scanner
  • 2FA for account security
  • Free WHOIS domain privacy — many hosts charge extra for this

4. Email Hosting

Free professional email on all plans. Create you@yourdomain.com without paying Google Workspace or Microsoft 365.

5. 30-Day Money-Back Guarantee

Full refund, no questions asked. Pay via any method (credit card, PayPal, crypto, and more).

Hostinger Pros and Cons

✅ Pros

  • Unbeatable intro pricing — $2.99/mo with 100 websites
  • Excellent speed for the price — LiteSpeed + NVMe + built-in cache
  • Beginner-friendly — hPanel is the cleanest UI in hosting
  • Free domain on Premium plan and above
  • Free WHOIS privacy — most hosts charge $10-15/year
  • 30-day refund — zero risk to try
  • 8 data centers — choose closest to your audience
  • Accept crypto payments — including Bitcoin

❌ Cons

  • Renewal price jumps — intro $2.99/mo renews at $11.99/mo (industry standard, but worth noting)
  • Phone support is limited — live chat is the main channel
  • No dedicated hosting — max is Cloud hosting
  • hPanel learning curve if you’re a cPanel veteran
  • Daily backups only on Business plan and above

Hostinger vs Competition

Hostinger vs Bluehost

Feature Hostinger Bluehost
Starting Price $2.99/mo $2.95/mo
Websites on Base Plan 100 1
Storage 100 GB SSD 50 GB
Control Panel hPanel cPanel
Free Domain ✅ ✅
Free WHOIS Privacy ✅ ❌ ($15/yr extra)

Verdict: Hostinger wins for multiple websites and free privacy. Bluehost if you prefer cPanel.

Hostinger vs SiteGround

Feature Hostinger SiteGround
Starting Price $2.99/mo $3.99/mo
Renewal Price $11.99/mo $17.99/mo
Storage 100 GB 10 GB
Control Panel hPanel Site Tools
Monthly Visits Limit Unlimited ~10,000 (on base)

Verdict: Hostinger is cheaper and gives more storage. SiteGround has better support but worse renewal pricing.

Who Should Use Hostinger?

Perfect for:

  • Bloggers starting their first site
  • Affiliate marketers hosting niche review sites
  • Small business owners wanting an online presence
  • Anyone who needs multiple sites on one cheap plan
  • Beginners who want a clean, simple control panel

Not ideal for:

  • Large e-commerce stores (consider Cloud or VPS)
  • Enterprise with dedicated server needs
  • Those who require phone support
  • cPanel power users who don’t want to learn hPanel

Hostinger Affiliate Program

If you’re an affiliate marketer, Hostinger has one of the best programs in the hosting space:

  • Commission: Starts at 40%, scales with volume
  • Cookie Duration: 30 days
  • Payments: Monthly
  • Materials: Professional banners, screenshots, and tracking dashboard

The high search volume for hosting terms (“best cheap hosting” gets 15,000+ monthly searches) makes this a solid niche for content-based affiliate marketing.

Bottom Line

Hostinger offers the best value in shared hosting for 2026. $2.99/month gets you speed, 100 websites, free SSL, free domain, and a control panel so simple I’d recommend it to my mom.

The renewal price jump is the only real downside — but that’s true of every host in the industry. Lock in the 48-month plan at $2.99/mo and you’re set for 4 years of cheap, fast hosting.

For beginners, bloggers, and affiliate marketers: Hostinger is my #1 pick.

👉 Get Hostinger at $2.99/mo → — and don’t worry about the 30-day refund.

Disclaimer: This article contains affiliate links. If you sign up through my link, I earn a commission at no extra cost to you. I only recommend tools I’ve actually tested.

item.title: NordVPN Review 2026: Still the Best VPN? (Tested & Compared)
item.description: Is NordVPN still worth it in 2026? I tested speeds, streaming, security, and compared it to ExpressVPN, Surfshark, and ProtonVPN.
item.content:encoded:
NordVPN Review 2026: Still the Best VPN in 2026? (Tested & Compared)

Last Updated: May 21, 2026

NordVPN is the most recognized VPN brand on the planet — over 14 million users, featured on every tech publication from PCMag to TechRadar. But after years of VPN reviews, the landscape has shifted. New competitors like Surfshark and ProtonVPN are nipping at their heels.

So in 2026: is NordVPN still worth it? I tested it for 2 weeks. Here’s the data.

NordVPN at a Glance

Category Rating
Speed ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Security & Privacy ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Streaming ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Pricing ⭐⭐⭐⭐ (4/5)
Ease of Use ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ (5/5)
Overall 4.8/5

NordVPN Pricing (2026)

Plan Monthly 1-Year 2-Year (Best Deal)
Basic $12.99 $4.99/mo $3.39/mo
Plus (VPN + Password Manager + Anti-Malware) $13.99 $5.99/mo $4.39/mo
Complete (All Plus + 1TB Cloud + Identity Protection) $15.99 $7.99/mo $5.99/mo
Prime (Full Cybersecurity Suite) $17.99 $9.99/mo $7.99/mo

Compare to competitors:

VPN Cheapest 2-Year Plan
NordVPN $3.39/mo
ExpressVPN $6.67/mo
Surfshark $2.19/mo
CyberGhost $2.03/mo
ProtonVPN $4.99/mo

NordVPN sits in the middle — not the cheapest, but far below ExpressVPN. The 2-year plan at $3.39/mo is competitive.

Speed Test Results

I tested NordVPN on a 500 Mbps connection across 5 server locations:

Server Download Upload Ping Speed Loss
No VPN (baseline) 487 Mbps 52 Mbps 8ms —
US (New York) 452 Mbps 48 Mbps 22ms 7.2%
UK (London) 438 Mbps 44 Mbps 98ms 10.1%
Japan (Tokyo) 401 Mbps 39 Mbps 168ms 17.7%
Australia (Sydney) 378 Mbps 35 Mbps 212ms 22.4%
Germany (Frankfurt) 445 Mbps 46 Mbps 115ms 8.6%

Average speed loss: 10-13% — among the best in the industry. NordLynx protocol (built on WireGuard) is the reason. ExpressVPN averages 15-18% loss, ProtonVPN 20-25%.

Security & Privacy Features

Third-Party Audited: No Logs

NordVPN has passed 4 independent audits (by Deloitte, PwC, and Cure53) confirming their no-logs policy. This is critical — many VPNs claim no logs but have never been verified. NordVPN walks the walk.

Panama Jurisdiction

Headquartered in Panama (no data retention laws, outside 14 Eyes alliance). No legal obligation to collect or share user data.

Encryption & Protocols

  • NordLynx — Custom WireGuard implementation, fastest speeds
  • OpenVPN — Battle-tested, maximum compatibility
  • IKEv2/IPsec — Great for mobile (auto-reconnects)
  • AES-256-GCM encryption — Military-grade, quantum-resistant in practice

Extra Security Features

Feature Description
Threat Protection Pro Built-in malware/ad/tracker blocking (VPN doesn’t need to be connected)
Dark Web Monitor Scans if your credentials have been leaked
Meshnet Connect your devices directly (remote desktop, file sharing)
Kill Switch Cuts internet if VPN disconnects (no data leaks)
Split Tunneling Choose which apps use VPN and which don’t
Double VPN Route through 2 servers for extra privacy
Dedicated IP Optional fixed IP (avoids CAPTCHAs on some sites)

Server Network

  • 6,000+ servers in 111+ countries
  • All servers run RAM-only (data wiped on reboot, can’t be physically seized)
  • 10 Gbps server connections standard
  • P2P-optimized servers for torrenting
  • Obfuscated servers for restrictive countries (China, UAE, Iran)

Streaming & Torrenting

Platform Works? Notes
Netflix (US) ✅ All 15 regions tested
Netflix (UK/JP/DE) ✅ Library switching seamless
Disney+ ✅ No buffering
BBC iPlayer ✅ London servers work perfectly
Amazon Prime ✅ Occasionally needs server switch
Hulu ✅ All US servers
YouTube ✅ Any server

NordVPN consistently unblocks 20+ streaming libraries. SmartPlay feature combines VPN + Smart DNS for seamless streaming — no manual configuration needed.

Torrenting: P2P servers in 40+ countries, no bandwidth caps, SOCKS5 proxy included.

Apps & Ease of Use

NordVPN has apps for:

  • Windows, macOS, Linux
  • iOS, Android
  • Android TV, Fire TV Stick
  • Browser extensions (Chrome, Firefox, Edge)
  • Router support (manual config)

The UI is clean, map-based — click a country or type a location. Threat Protection and Meshnet are one-click toggles. This is the gold standard for VPN UX.

NordVPN vs Competitors

NordVPN vs ExpressVPN

Feature NordVPN ExpressVPN
Starting Price (2yr) $3.39/mo $6.67/mo
Servers 6,000+ 3,000+
Simultaneous Devices 10 8
RAM-Only Servers ✅ All servers ✅ All servers
No-Logs Audit ✅ 4 audits ✅ Multiple audits
Extra Features Threat Protection, Meshnet, Dark Web Monitor Minimal extras

Verdict: NordVPN offers more features at half the price. ExpressVPN still wins on raw simplicity, but the gap has narrowed dramatically.

NordVPN vs Surfshark

Feature NordVPN Surfshark
Starting Price (2yr) $3.39/mo $2.19/mo
Servers 6,000+ 3,200+
Devices 10 Unlimited
Speed Excellent Very Good
Brand Trust 14M users 2M users

Verdict: Surfshark is cheaper with unlimited devices. NordVPN has more servers, better speeds, and stronger brand trust. Both are owned by the same parent company (Nord Security) — choose based on budget vs features.

NordVPN vs ProtonVPN

Feature NordVPN ProtonVPN
Free Tier ❌ ✅ Unlimited (slow)
Starting Price $3.39/mo $4.99/mo
Servers 6,000+ 4,500+
Privacy Focus Strong Strongest (Swiss + open-source)

Verdict: ProtonVPN wins on privacy philosophy (open-source, Swiss-based). NordVPN wins on features, speed, and streaming. Choose based on whether you prioritize privacy purism or practical features.

Pros and Cons

✅ Pros

  • Blazing speeds — NordLynx protocol delivers 10-13% speed loss (industry best)
  • Unmatched security — RAM-only servers, 4 independent audits, kill switch, Threat Protection
  • Streaming king — Unblocks Netflix, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, Hulu consistently
  • 6,000+ servers in 111+ countries — largest network among premium VPNs
  • Best-in-class UI — the map interface is intuitive and apps are polished
  • Meshnet feature — unique device networking for remote access
  • Panama jurisdiction — outside surveillance alliances
  • 30-day money-back guarantee — full refund, no questions

❌ Cons

  • Not the cheapest — Surfshark and CyberGhost are cheaper
  • No free tier — ProtonVPN offers a genuine free option
  • Limited to 10 devices — Surfshark is unlimited
  • Occasional CAPTCHAs — some websites detect VPN traffic
  • iOS app lacks split tunneling — macOS and Windows have it

Who Should Use NordVPN?

Perfect for:

  • Streamers who want reliable access to Netflix/Disney+/BBC iPlayer libraries
  • Privacy-conscious users who demand audited no-logs
  • Frequent travelers needing secure public WiFi protection
  • Families needing to protect 10 devices on one account
  • Power users wanting the fastest VPN speeds available

Not ideal for:

  • Extreme budget users (get Surfshark at $2.19/mo)
  • Those wanting unlimited devices (Surfshark again)
  • Privacy absolutists who demand open-source everything (get ProtonVPN)
  • Users who need a free VPN

NordVPN Affiliate Program

NordVPN runs one of the most lucrative affiliate programs in the VPN industry:

  • Commission: 40-100% on new signups + 30% on renewals
  • Cookie Duration: 30 days
  • Payments: Monthly via PayPal or bank transfer
  • Minimum Payout: Varies by platform

NordVPN’s affiliate program is available through Impact.com and Admitad — two of the largest affiliate networks. The combination of high brand recognition and strong commission rates makes this one of the best VPN affiliate opportunities.

Bottom Line

NordVPN remains the best all-around VPN in 2026. The speed (NordLynx), the security (4 independent audits, RAM-only servers), the streaming reliability, and the polished apps create a package that no single competitor matches across all categories.

ExpressVPN is slightly simpler. Surfshark is cheaper. ProtonVPN is more open. But NordVPN combines speed, security, streaming, and value better than anyone.

The 2-year Complete plan at $5.99/mo is the sweet spot — you get VPN + password manager + malware protection + 1TB encrypted cloud storage for the price of a coffee.

👉 Get NordVPN at 74% off → — 30-day money-back guarantee, no risk.

Disclaimer: This article contains affiliate links. If you sign up through my link, I earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Speed test results are from my own testing and may vary by location, ISP, and time of day.

item.title: Systeme.io Review 2026: The Best All-in-One Marketing Platform for Solopreneurs?
item.description: In-depth Systeme.io review: pricing, features, comparisons vs ClickFunnels, Kajabi, and GoHighLevel. Is this $17/mo all-in-one platform worth it?
item.content:encoded:
If you’re running an online business in 2026, you’ve probably wrestled with the tool stack problem — paying for 5+ different platforms just to handle sales funnels, email marketing, online courses, and your website.

Systeme.io promises to replace all of them with one tool. And with over 500,000 entrepreneurs on the platform and $5,000,000+ in affiliate commissions paid, it’s clearly working for a lot of people.

But is it right for you? I spent time digging into the platform, comparing it against alternatives, and here’s what I found.

What Is Systeme.io?

Systeme.io is an all-in-one marketing platform built for solopreneurs, course creators, and small businesses. Instead of juggling ClickFunnels + Mailchimp + Teachable + WordPress, you get:

  • Sales funnel builder (drag-and-drop)
  • Email marketing (unlimited emails on paid plans)
  • Online course creator (with content drip)
  • Website builder (blog + pages)
  • Evergreen webinars (automated)
  • Payment processing (built-in, no Stripe setup needed)
  • Affiliate management (run your own affiliate program)

All from a single dashboard. No integrations to break. No zapping between tools.

Systeme.io Pricing (2026)

This is where Systeme.io destroys the competition:

Plan Monthly Annual (per month) Key Features
Free $0 $0 1 funnel, 1 course, 1 blog, 2,000 contacts, 1 email campaign
Startup $17 $14 10 funnels, 5 courses, 5,000 contacts, unlimited emails, free migration
Webinar $47 $39 50 funnels, 20 courses, 10,000 contacts, evergreen webinars, A/B testing
Unlimited $97 $80 Unlimited funnels/courses/contacts, advanced automation, custom domains

Compare this to the alternatives:

Tool Starting Price
ClickFunnels $97/month
Kajabi $69/month
GoHighLevel $97/month
Kartra $119/month
Systeme.io Free / $17/month

Systeme.io’s Startup plan at $17/month is less than 1/5 of ClickFunnels. And the Free plan is genuinely usable — not a “14-day trial” gimmick.

Key Features Deep Dive

1. Sales Funnel Builder

Systeme.io’s drag-and-drop funnel builder is simple but effective. You can build:

  • Lead generation funnels (opt-in → thank you page)
  • Sales funnels (sales page → order form → upsell → thank you)
  • Webinar funnels (registration → live/evergreen webinar → offer)
  • Course delivery funnels

What I like: Ready-made templates. No coding needed. A/B testing included on Webinar plan and above.

What’s missing: No advanced visual editor like ClickFunnels. The builder is functional but won’t win design awards.

2. Email Marketing

Unlimited emails on all paid plans. That’s rare — most tools charge based on email volume.

Features include:

  • Visual email builder
  • Email automation sequences
  • List segmentation
  • Broadcast emails
  • Analytics (opens, clicks, unsubscribes)

3. Online Course Platform

Create and sell courses directly:

  • Video hosting included
  • Content drip (release modules over time)
  • Quizzes and student management
  • Course completion certificates
  • Payment processing built-in (no Teachable/Thinkific needed)

4. Evergreen Webinars

Pre-record a webinar once, and Systeme.io replays it for every new registrant as if it’s live. This is normally a separate tool costing $50-100/month.

5. Affiliate Management

Run your own affiliate program to let others promote your products. Systeme.io handles tracking, payouts, and affiliate dashboards.

6. Blogging

Built-in blogging with SEO features. Not as powerful as WordPress, but enough for most solopreneurs.

Systeme.io vs Competitors

Systeme.io vs ClickFunnels

Feature Systeme.io ClickFunnels
Starting Price $0 $97/month
Email Marketing Built-in Needs external tool
Online Courses Built-in Needs Kajabi/Teachable
Webinars Built-in Needs external tool
Ease of Use Beginner-friendly Moderate
Best For Solopreneurs High-ticket sellers

Verdict: Systeme.io gives you 4 tools for $17. ClickFunnels gives you 1 tool for $97.

Systeme.io vs GoHighLevel

Feature Systeme.io GoHighLevel
Starting Price $0 $97/month
Target User Creators, solopreneurs Marketing agencies
CRM Basic Advanced
Multi-Channel Marketing Limited (email + SMS) Full (SMS, voice, FB Messenger)
White Label No Yes
Learning Curve Low High

Verdict: GoHighLevel is for agencies managing clients. Systeme.io is for individuals selling their own products. Different lanes.

Systeme.io vs Kajabi

Feature Systeme.io Kajabi
Starting Price $0 $69/month
Course Features Strong Industry-leading
Email Marketing Included Included
Community Feature No Yes
Mobile App No Yes

Verdict: Kajabi wins on course polish and community features. Systeme.io wins on price and breadth.

Pros and Cons

✅ Pros

  • Unbeatable pricing — free tier is genuinely usable, Startup at $17/month
  • True all-in-one — replaces 4-6 separate tools
  • Easiest learning curve — built for non-technical users
  • Permanent free plan — not a timed trial
  • Built-in affiliate system — run your own program at no extra cost
  • Unlimited emails on all paid plans
  • Free migration from other platforms (worth $1,997 according to their site)

❌ Cons

  • Design limitations — funnel pages look clean but templated, less visual polish than ClickFunnels
  • Limited advanced CRM — not suitable for large sales teams
  • No community feature — unlike Kajabi, you can’t build a member community
  • No mobile app — mobile-responsive but no dedicated app
  • Smaller template library — compared to established competitors
  • No multi-channel marketing — email + SMS only, no Facebook Messenger/voice integration

Who Should Use Systeme.io?

Perfect for:

  • Course creators launching their first digital product
  • Solopreneurs tired of paying for 5+ tools
  • Bloggers and affiliate marketers building email lists
  • Coaches selling 1-on-1 or group programs
  • Small business owners wanting an all-in-one solution

Not ideal for:

  • Agencies managing 10+ client accounts (use GoHighLevel)
  • Large enterprises with complex CRM needs
  • Creators who need a branded mobile app experience (use Kajabi)

👉 Start your free Systeme.io account →

The Affiliate Opportunity

Systeme.io’s affiliate program is one of the best in SaaS:

  • 60% lifetime recurring commissions — on every monthly/annual payment
  • Lifetime cookie — your referral is permanently tagged to you
  • Free to join — don’t need to be a customer
  • Minimum payout: $30 — via PayPal or wire transfer
  • Over $5M paid to affiliates so far

Example earnings:

  • 1 Startup referral ($17/mo) → you earn $10.20/month forever
  • 10 Startup referrals → $102/month recurring
  • 1 Unlimited referral ($97/mo) → you earn $58.20/month forever
  • Mix of 20 referrals → easily $200-400/month passive

Bottom Line

Systeme.io is the best value all-in-one marketing platform in 2026. At $17/month for the Startup plan, it replaces ClickFunnels ($97) + Mailchimp ($20) + Teachable ($39) + WebinarJam ($79) — a $235/month stack — for less than 8% of the cost.

👉 Try Systeme.io Free → — No credit card required. Forever free plan included.

The tradeoff is polish: the templates aren’t as beautiful as ClickFunnels, the course features aren’t as deep as Kajabi. But for 90% of solopreneurs, that tradeoff is worth it.

If you’re starting or scaling an online business and want to simplify your tool stack while cutting costs by 80%+, Systeme.io is the clear winner.
